page: Amazon Web Services c7gd.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by 64 physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton processor architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz with a dedicated core allocation and no hyperthreading, this instance delivers 128 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It features a substantial 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks highlight the server's strong multi-threaded capabilities and memory subsystem. The system achieves top-tier results in multi-core compilation tasks and Passmark memory tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth metrics, reaching 277.0 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also highly rated, with Redis operations exceeding 102 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation speeds represent relative tradeoffs, performing in the average to poor ranges respectively.

Given its hardware profile, the c7gd.metal instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that benefit from physical core isolation and direct hardware access without virtualization overhead. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, high-throughput database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton processor architecture running at 2.6 GHz. Equipped with 128 GB of DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, this instance delivers high-throughput performance without hyperthreading. Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core execution, particularly in software compilation and database operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth. While single-core speeds and small-model text generation represent performance tradeoffs, the server's 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and direct hardware access provide excellent resource density. It is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads, database hosting, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 physical ARMv8 cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database and multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal ARMv8 platform combining sixty-four dedicated physical cores with high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated physical ARMv8 cores at 2.6 GHz with no hyperthreading
Equipped with 128 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier performance in software compilation and database operations
Provides 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and static web serving
